ontology.foodex2_description: Sandwiches and similar filled bread products
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers sandwiches and filled bread products; used as the closest defensible category for a filled bagel dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No sufficiently specific SNOMED CT concept identified for chicken bagel with cream cheese; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is approximately 150 g, representing a single bagel filled with chicken and cream cheese as commonly sold in Singapore cafes and delis.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Bagel With Cream Cheese — Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for Chicken Bagel With Cream Cheese (cr_id G00010921). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are sourced from this record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central cream cheese and chicken breast composition data as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this composite dish; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for chicken bagel with cream cheese. GI estimated at 72 based on the GI of a plain white bagel (GI ~72, University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). The chicken and cream cheese components contribute negligible carbohydrates and do not materially alter the GI of the bagel base. Glycemic load per 150 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (72 × 13.3) / 100 ≈ 9.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Extremely high sodium at 1279 mg/100 g, far exceeding the 600 mg/100 g threshold for high-sodium foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; a 150 g serving delivers approximately 1919 mg sodium, representing over 80% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg, and the high sodium load cannot be offset by the modest potassium content of 295 mg/100 g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index bagel base (GI ~72) combined with zero dietary fibre and 6.24 g/100 g sugar, producing a high glycaemic response; saturated fat from cream cheese worsens insulin resistance
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this dish in its standard form; if consumed, substitute a wholegrain bagel to lower the GI and add fibre, reduce cream cheese to limit saturated fat, and pair with non-starchy vegetables to blunt the postprandial glucose spike.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~9.8 g/100 g), trace trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g), very high sodium (1279 mg/100 g), and elevated cholesterol (78 mg/100 g) collectively raise cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this dish due to its combination of high saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium; if consumed occasionally, request reduced cream cheese, choose a wholegrain bagel, and limit portion size to reduce atherogenic and hypertensive burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat (~9.8 g/100 g) and trace trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g) from cream cheese are pro-inflammatory; high sodium (1279 mg/100 g) worsens joint swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish; cream cheese should be replaced with anti-inflammatory alternatives such as avocado or hummus, and individuals should monitor tolerance to tomato as a nightshade ingredient that may trigger flares in sensitive patients.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Bagel with Cream Cheese is a filled bread sandwich consisting of a plain white bagel spread generously with cream cheese and topped with cooked chicken, commonly sold in Singapore cafes, delis, and fast-casual outlets. The bagel provides a dense, chewy bread base with a slightly glossy crust, while the cream cheese contributes a rich, tangy, and fatty filling that contrasts with the savoury, tender chicken. Per 100 g, the dish delivers 307 kcal, 16.1 g protein, 23.1 g fat (of which approximately 9.8 g is saturated fat and trace trans fat from the cream cheese), and 8.9 g carbohydrate with zero dietary fibre. Sodium is extremely high at 1279 mg/100 g, making this dish unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. The high glycaemic index of the white bagel base (~72) combined with the absence of dietary fibre creates a rapid postprandial glucose response. Calcium is notably high at 1375 mg/100 g, largely attributable to the cream cheese component. This dish is not vegetarian, not vegan, contains gluten and dairy allergens, and is not confirmed halal. It is typically consumed as a lunch or dinner item in Singapore and is classified as a processed chicken-based dish within the meat and alternatives food group.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95 for an exact named dish match (cr_id G00010921). Saturated fat and trans fat are estimated from USDA FoodData Central cream cheese and chicken breast composition data and clearly documented as estimates. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for white bagel, which is the dominant carbohydrate contributor.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (9.8 g/100 g) and trans fat (0.1 g/100 g) are not reported directly by HPB for this composite dish; values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries for cream cheese (USDA FDC ID 173417) and cooked chicken breast, weighted by approximate ingredient proportions. These are clearly marked as estimates. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions and full micronutrient panel for this composite dish are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as a combined prepared dish entry as of 2026-04-16. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as reported by HPB for this dish; the white bagel base and cream cheese contribute negligible fibre, confirmed zero per HPB source. 4. Calcium at 1375 mg/100 g is unusually high and is preserved as reported by HPB; this likely reflects the cream cheese component which is a concentrated dairy calcium source. 5. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on white bagel GI from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008); no direct GI measurement for this composite dish exists in approved sources. 6. Halal status is not confirmed; cream cheese sourcing and chicken preparation method are not specified in the HPB record.
